Newsletters





The Summer Olympics opened Friday, July 26.

The Summer Olympics opened Friday, July 26.

This article is over a year old, and the information within may be out of date.

The Google Doodle on Friday, July 26 celebrates the opening of the Paris Olympic Games.

Friday’s Google Doodle is all about celebrating the opening of the Summer Olympics.

Titled “Paris Games Begin Doodle,” the animated graphic on Google’s search page launched the same day that the Paris Games officially opened on July 26.

The Doodle features five birds floating down a river through a city, inspired by the Paris Games’ opening ceremony, which will feature athletes floating down the Seine.

The same Doodle appears on the Google pages in many countries all over the world.

You can learn more about the Doodleonline.

The Olympics theme is expected to continue on Saturday, with an animated Doodle featuring birds doing skateboarding tricks in a halfpipe.

According to Google, its first Doodle was an out-of-office message published in 1998 to let people know Google founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin would be away for the Burning Man festival.

Since then, Google has created more than 5,000 Doodles. A notable early days Doodle celebratedHalloweenin 2000, and the first interactive game Doodle celebrated the 30th anniversary ofPac-Manin 2010. In 2021, Google released what it called the largest multiplayer Doodle ever, theDoodles Champion Islandgame.

Read more about the history of Google Doodles athttps://doodles.google/about/.
